86.reason: [If the list has only one node or is empty, p->next will be NULL, causing a segmentation fault when accessing p->next in the loop condition. Also, the sorting logic is flawed鈥攊t doesn't implement a correct bubble sort and may access NULL pointers during comparison.]

87.reason: [This inner loop starts from the second node every time, which breaks standard bubble sort logic. Additionally, if *head is NULL (empty list), (*head)->next would dereference a NULL pointer, leading to undefined behavior.]
